metadata.title: Planck Blackbody Radiation Data — Temperature to Color Mapping
metadata.description: Complete blackbody radiation database including RGB colors for temperatures from 1000K to 100000K in 100K increments. CIE 1931 2-degree standard observer color matching functions with sRGB gamma correction.

u^3}{c^2}  rac{1}{e^{h
u/k_BT} - 1}

radianceExitance.note: Factor of π relates specific intensity (radiance) to exitance for isotropic radiation

accretionDiskTemperatureZones.description: Temperature ranges for different regions of a black hole accretion disk
accretionDiskTemperatureZones.massScaling: T_max ∝ M^(-1/4) — more massive BHs have cooler disks

examples.note: Too hot for visible light — disk appears blue-white if redshifted
Inner disk is UV; visible portions are blue-white

sources: CIE 1931 2-degree Standard Observer Color Matching Functions
Wyman, Sloan & Shirley, JCGT 2(2), 1-11 (2013) — Analytic approximations to CIE XYZ
Ballesteros, EPL 97, 34008 (2012) — Color index to effective temperature
Harre & Heller, Astronomische Nachrichten 342, 578 (2021) — True colors of stars
